title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — July 6, 2026
headline: Russia's July civilian toll reaches 170 per day — the highest rate since 2023 — as the Kyiv barrage kills 21-plus with zero of 23 missiles intercepted; El Salvador's Critical-band score of 15.0 of 100 is now published and the pending queue is clear.
summary: July 6, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The pending queue entered July 6 empty — El Salvador's downgrade to 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band was applied on July 5, 2026, the first score change since the June series. Russia's July civilian-casualty rate has reached 170 per day — the highest sustained pace since 2023 — as the July 5-6 Kyiv barrage killed 21-plus people with zero of 23 missiles intercepted, and a Dnipro strike killed 17 more. Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100. Sudan holds at 0 of 100 as Al Jazeera's July 6 reporting describes El Obeid as potentially the next site following the El Fasher atrocity pattern — with 500,000 people at imminent siege risk in North Kordofan's state capital. The Democratic Republic of the Congo's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak recorded its fastest 3-day case growth: the WHO July 6 count reached 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths, up from 1,406 and 438 three days earlier. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 with 141 June executions now on record and the Khamenei burial scheduled for July 9. Haiti holds at 4.7 of 100 as the Temporary Protected Status work-authorization for approximately 350,000 Haitians expires July 10 — a United States-conduct forward trigger. Israel enters the July priority cluster for the first time as a perpetrator own-conduct entity, confirmed at 0 of 100, on the basis of the July 6 al-Mawasi drone strike on displaced people and the Nabatieh car strike in Lebanon. Fifteen false-positive screens were documented for the fourth consecutive cycle — the longest record-tied streak in the benchmark's history.

pipeline.elSalvadorHandling: El Salvador downgrade (20.3 to 15.0, Developing to Critical, delta -5.3) was applied on 2026-07-05. Published score is now 15.0 of 100, Critical band, rank 155. El Salvador dropped from the priority cluster. The pending queue entered July 6 empty and exits empty. This was the first applied score change since the June series.

topSignals.title: Russia 0 of 100: July Civilian Toll Hits 170 Per Day — Highest Since 2023; Kyiv Jul 5-6 Kills 21-Plus, Zero of 23 Missiles Intercepted; Dnipro Strike Kills 17; EU Special Tribunal Active; Ukraine 50.0 of 100 Confirmed; South Sudan 0 of 100 — Famine Risk in Four Counties
topSignals.whyItMatters: Russia's July civilian-casualty rate is 170 per day — the highest since 2023. The Kyiv barrage intercepted zero of 23 missiles. Russia holds at 0 of 100. Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 on its own civilian-protection conduct.
topSignals.description: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor, reinforced for the sixth consecutive cycle in the July series. The July 5-6 Kyiv barrage killed 21-plus civilians with zero of 23 missiles intercepted. The simultaneous Dnipro strike killed 17. That is 38-plus confirmed dead in the July 5-6 window. The July daily civilian-casualty rate has reached 170 per day — the highest sustained pace since 2023. By this measure, July 2026 is the most severe month in the benchmark's tracking period for Russia-attributed civilian targeting. The EU crime-of-aggression Special Tribunal is operational. 20,500-plus children have been documented as forcibly deported. Russia cannot go lower than 0 of 100. Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 in the Functional band. Ukraine's civilian-protection response — coordinated metro-sheltering and evacuation during the strike campaign — is consistent with the Functional band. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by one state on another is scored against the source state. Russia's deliberate targeting is Russia's scored conduct; Ukraine's protection measures are Ukraine's scored conduct. South Sudan holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. 7.8 million people are food insecure; 73,300 are at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — a 160 percent increase since June; famine risk is confirmed in four counties. A cross-border Ebola incursion risk from DRC's Haut-Uele province is a forward trigger for South Sudan but is itself exogenous. Confirmed at 0 of 100 for Russia, 50.0 of 100 for Ukraine, and 0 of 100 for South Sudan; confidence: high for all three.

evidence.claim: OHCHR Ukraine Monitoring Mission July 2026: July civilian-casualty rate at 170 per day — the highest sustained pace since 2023; July 5-6 Kyiv barrage killed 21-plus civilians with zero of 23 missiles intercepted; Dnipro strike killed 17; deliberate targeting of residential areas and energy infrastructure attributed to Russian forces.
IPC July 2026: South Sudan — 7.8 million food insecure; 73,300 people at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in four counties; famine risk confirmed; 160 percent increase in Phase 5 population since June.

topSignals.title: El Salvador 15.0 of 100 Critical — Downgrade Applied July 5; Published Score Is Now 15.0; Pending Queue Clear; First Apply Since June Series; China 19.5 of 100 — Article 63 Day 6 Without Enforcement
topSignals.whyItMatters: El Salvador's score is now 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band — the downgrade was applied on July 5. The pending queue is clear. China's Ethnic Unity Law diaspora clause has been in force six days without a documented enforcement action.
topSignals.description: El Salvador holds at 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band. The July 2, 2026 proposal to move El Salvador from 20.3 of 100 (Developing band) to 15.0 of 100 (Critical band) — a delta of -5.3 — was applied on July 5, 2026. This is the score El Salvador now carries in all public-facing systems. The proposal was based on: Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization after 25 years — forced into exile under prosecution threat; the indefinite presidential re-election constitutional amendment enacted August 2025 by a 57-to-3 vote; 86 documented political prisoners; child life-sentences; and 140-plus rights defenders who have fled the country. El Salvador drops from the July priority cluster. This is the first applied score change since the June series — the prior apply was Humana on June 21, 2026. The pending queue entered July 6 empty and exits July 6 empty. China holds at 19.5 of 100 in the Critical band. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law's Article 63 diaspora liability has been in force for six days without a documented enforcement action — no prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member has been confirmed in Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, or the United States. The sub-threshold composite dock of approximately -1.5 points does not cross the change threshold without an enforcement event. China at 19.5 of 100 sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0.

evidence.claim: Al Jazeera August 2025: El Salvador's Legislative Assembly approved a constitutional amendment allowing indefinite presidential re-election with 57 votes in favor and three opposed; presidential term extended to six years; runoff removed.
NPR July 2025: Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization after 25 years of operation — forced out of the country under threat of prosecution.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0 of 100: Al Jazeera Reports El Obeid as Potentially the Next El Fasher — UN Red Alert Day 3; RSF Siege Active; DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Reaches 1,561 Cases and 506 Deaths — Fastest 3-Day Growth in Outbreak History; July Peak Window Active
topSignals.whyItMatters: Sudan's El Obeid siege is drawing direct comparisons to El Fasher — the site where crimes against humanity were documented. Sudan holds at 0 of 100. The DRC's Ebola outbreak recorded its fastest 3-day growth, with 1,561 cases and 506 deaths. DRC holds at 2.3 of 100.
topSignals.description: Sudan holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor, reinforced for the fourth consecutive cycle. Al Jazeera's July 6 report frames El Obeid as potentially the next El Fasher, documenting a pattern match with the site that produced Amnesty International's crimes-against-humanity finding in the July series. The UN red alert issued July 3 is now three days on record: 500,000 people face imminent siege risk in North Kordofan's state capital alongside 105,000 internally displaced persons in surrounding zones. Al-Burhan has not accepted a ceasefire. The RSF's El Obeid offensive is non-state conduct. Sudan at 0 of 100 reflects the Sudanese government's own prior conduct: the breakdown of state protection and documented rejection of ceasefire proposals. The scored upgrade trigger — an implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access — has not occurred. The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) holds at 2.3 of 100 — near-floor Critical. The WHO July 6 update recorded 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths — up from 1,406 cases and 438 deaths on July 3. The 155-case 3-day increment is the fastest in the outbreak's history. The outbreak is in the July natural Bundibugyo seasonal peak window, active through July 31. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine exists. Contact tracing stands at approximately 45 percent. Multi-continent spread to Uganda, France, and Germany was confirmed in the prior cycle. The floor-crossing trigger — documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. Confirmed at 0 of 100 for Sudan; confidence: high. Confirmed at 2.3 of 100 for DRC; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: Al Jazeera July 6, 2026: RSF El Obeid siege draws direct comparison to El Fasher pattern; UN red alert active since July 3; 500,000 people at imminent siege risk in North Kordofan state capital; Sudan government has not accepted ceasefire.

WHO July 6, 2026: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak at 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths under active PHEIC; 155-case 3-day increment is fastest in outbreak history; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; July natural seasonal peak window active; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 of 100: 141 June Executions on Record; Khamenei Burial July 9 — Watch Window Three Days Out; 784-Plus YTD at 37-Year High; US-Iran Talks Resume July 11; Haiti 4.7 of 100: TPS Work-Authorization Expires July 10 — 350,000 Affected
topSignals.whyItMatters: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 with 141 June executions now on record as the Khamenei burial approaches July 9. Haiti holds at 4.7 of 100 as work authorization for approximately 350,000 Haitians expires July 10 — a United States-conduct event, not scored against Haiti.
topSignals.description: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 — near-floor Critical. 141 executions in June 2026 are now on record — the highest monthly figure in the priced 784-plus year-to-date count (the highest annual rate in 37 years). The Khamenei burial is scheduled for July 9 — three days away. The protest-suppression watch flag remains active through July 9. A documented lethal crackdown on mourners, demonstrators, or political rivals during the July 4-9 funeral window constitutes a new scored harm event. Under the double-count rule, the 141 June executions and the Fallahi death sentence (July 1) continue the already-priced 784-plus annual pattern — a single month's count does not open a new scored threshold on its own. US-Iran Doha nuclear talks are scheduled to resume July 11. Haiti holds at 4.7 of 100 — near-floor Critical. The Temporary Protected Status work-authorization for approximately 350,000 Haitians expires July 10 — four days away. This is United States conduct, scored against the United States under the benchmark's attribution rule — not against Haiti. The Haitian government did not cause the TPS termination. Gang control of approximately 90 percent of Port-au-Prince — with 8,200-plus people killed year-to-date — is non-state conduct. 5.8 million people are food insecure and 1.5 million are displaced. No fresh Haitian-state own-conduct event was identified in the July 6 window. The July 10 TPS expiration is the next concrete US-conduct checkpoint in this arc. Confirmed at 2.5 of 100 for Iran; confidence: high. Confirmed at 4.7 of 100 for Haiti; confidence: medium.

evidence.claim: Iran Human Rights Organization July 2026: 141 executions in June 2026 — the monthly total now on record; 784-plus executions year-to-date at a 37-year high; Sina Fallahi death sentence issued July 1; Khamenei burial scheduled July 9; US-Iran Doha talks to resume July 11.

OCHA Haiti July 2026: 5.8 million food insecure; 1.5 million displaced; gang control of approximately 90 percent of Port-au-Prince; 8,200-plus killed year-to-date by gang violence — non-state conduct; TPS work-authorization expires July 10 for approximately 350,000 Haitians — United States conduct, not scored against Haiti.

topSignals.title: Israel 0 of 100: New Priority Cluster Entrant on Perpetrator Own-Conduct — Jul 6 al-Mawasi Drone Strike on Displaced People; Lebanon Nabatieh Car Strike; Floor Confirmed; Palestine 25.0 of 100 Confirmed; Venezuela Quake Day 12 at 3,300-Plus Dead; 15 False-Positive Screens — Fourth Consecutive Record-Tied Cycle
topSignals.whyItMatters: Israel enters the July cluster on its own perpetrator conduct and is confirmed at 0 of 100. Palestine holds at 25.0 of 100 — harm from Israeli strikes is attributed to Israel, not Palestine. Venezuela reaches Day 12 with 3,300-plus dead. The benchmark has now screened 60 false positives across four consecutive nights without an incorrect score move.
topSignals.description: Israel holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor — and enters the July priority cluster for the first time as a perpetrator own-conduct entity. The July 6 al-Mawasi drone strike targeted displaced people in a humanitarian zone in Gaza. The Nabatieh car strike in Lebanon killed four people. Both events are Israel's own realized conduct, correctly scored against Israel as the perpetrator. The al-Mawasi drone strike would constitute a scored downgrade trigger for any entity above its floor. Israel is already at the 0 of 100 floor; these events reinforce but cannot lower the floor. Israel's entry clarifies the attribution architecture for this cluster: harm perpetrated by Israel is scored against Israel; Lebanon (17.2 of 100) is scored on Lebanon's own state conduct; Palestine (25.0 of 100) is scored on Palestinian-authority own conduct. Palestine holds at 25.0 of 100. The July 6 killing of six Palestinians is Israel's conduct, scored against Israel under the directionality rule. Hamas's announced dissolution of its Gaza government is a procedural and unrealized handover — the committee remains outside Gaza; members have described it as a formality. Absence of new Palestinian-state own-harm is not an upgrade signal; absence of new own-harm is not scored as positive conduct. Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100. The official death toll from the June 24 earthquake reached 3,300-plus on Day 12 with approximately 50,000 people unaccounted. The July 8 two-week checkpoint is two days out. Acceptance of external aid from international partners continues. Fifteen false-positive screens were documented for the fourth consecutive cycle — the first four-cycle record streak in the benchmark's history. All seven false-positive category types remain simultaneously active for the fourth straight night. 60 screens across four cycles with zero incorrect score movements.

evidence.claim: Al Jazeera July 6, 2026: Israeli drone strike on al-Mawasi humanitarian zone in Gaza targets displaced people — perpetrator conduct scored against Israel, not against Palestinian authority; Lebanon Nabatieh car strike kills four; both events are Israeli own-conduct.

OCHA oPt July 2026: July 6 killing of six Palestinians — attributable to Israeli forces under the directionality rule; Hamas governance dissolution announcement is procedural — committee remains outside Gaza; called a formality with members staying in position.

A further human-rights-defender imprisonment or mass-detention escalation constitutes additional downward pressure from the Critical band.

emergingRisks.risk: Russia's 170 civilian casualties per day in July 2026 is the highest sustained pace since 2023. The July 5-6 Kyiv barrage intercepted zero of 23 missiles and killed 21-plus. The Dnipro strike killed 17 more in the same window.
emergingRisks.detail: Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. The July pace, if sustained, would produce approximately 1,190 civilian casualties by month end — a pace without precedent in this benchmark's tracking period. Ukraine at 50.0 of 100 holds on its own civilian-protection conduct. The EU Special Tribunal is operational. 20,500-plus children deported remain on record. An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is the sole scored upgrade trigger. OCHA's El Obeid documentation is the next evidence checkpoint.

The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. A South Sudan Ebola incursion from Haut-Uele province combined with documented South Sudan own-conduct failure would be the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

emergingRisks.risk: Iran's Khamenei burial window closes July 9 — three days away. 141 June executions are now on record as the highest monthly total in the priced arc. US-Iran talks resume July 11.
emergingRisks.detail: Iran at 2.5 of 100 near-floor Critical. 784-plus executions year-to-date at a 37-year high. The 141 June executions continue the priced annual pattern under the double-count rule. A documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures during the July 4-9 funeral window, constitutes a new scored harm event. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Haiti's TPS work-authorization expires July 10 — four days away. Approximately 350,000 Haitians in the United States lose work authorization. This is United States conduct tracked in the benchmark's attribution framework.
emergingRisks.detail: Haiti at 4.7 of 100 Critical. The TPS termination was the SCOTUS Mullin v. Doe ruling — scored against the United States. The July 10 work-authorization expiration is the next concrete US-conduct forward checkpoint. Gang control of approximately 90 percent of Port-au-Prince is non-state. No fresh Haitian-state own-conduct event was identified. Documented Haitian-state security-force atrocities or deliberate relief obstruction remains the Haiti-scored downgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's earthquake toll has reached 3,300-plus dead on Day 12. The July 8 two-week checkpoint arrives in two days — the standard mark for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction.
emergingRisks.detail: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical, 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Official toll 3,300-plus on Day 12; approximately 50,000 unaccounted; $37 billion damage versus $200 million national fund. Aid acceptance from international partners continues; domestic distribution remains slow. The July 8 checkpoint is the deciding assessment for the Action and Accountability watch flags.

emergingRisks.risk: China's Article 63 diaspora enforcement watch is in its sixth day without a documented enforcement action since the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1.
emergingRisks.detail: China at 19.5 of 100, 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States are the observable proxies for first enforcement. The sub-threshold dock of approximately -1.5 composite points does not cross the change threshold without enforcement documentation.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 27 days away. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding constitutes a scored event. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented at 59.1 Functional. OpenAI and xAI compliance gaps will become legally significant on August 2. The EU AI Act is the next sector-wide scored deadline for the AI Labs Index.

emergingRisks.risk: Nigeria's August IPC lean-season update — currently tracking 35-million-plus food insecure — is the next checkpoint for a Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno with documented state aid obstruction.
emergingRisks.detail: Nigeria at 18.0 of 100 Critical, 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Record lean-season hunger. JNIM/ISWAP attacks are non-state. WFP funding gap is not state obstruction. Formal IPC Phase 5 declaration in Borno with documented state obstruction is the downgrade trigger. August lean-season update is the next evidence checkpoint.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Russia: Record July Pace; 170 Per Day Civilian Casualties; Zero of 23 Missiles Intercepted
sectorAlerts.headline: Russia's July civilian-casualty rate stands at 170 per day — the highest since 2023 — as the Kyiv barrage kills 21-plus with zero of 23 missiles intercepted and Dnipro kills 17; Russia holds at 0 of 100.
observations: Russia (0 of 100): The July 5-6 Kyiv barrage killed 21-plus civilians with zero of 23 missiles intercepted. The simultaneous Dnipro strike killed 17. That is 38-plus confirmed dead in the July 5-6 window — the highest two-day total in the July series.

The July daily civilian-casualty rate stands at 170 per day — the highest since 2023. By this quantitative measure, July 2026 is the most severe month in the benchmark's tracking period for Russia-attributed civilian targeting. The EU crime-of-aggression Special Tribunal is operational. 20,500-plus children forcibly deported remain on record.

Russia at 0 of 100 cannot go lower. Ukraine at 50.0 of 100 holds on its own civilian-protection conduct. The attribution rule functions correctly: Russia's deliberate targeting is Russia's scored harm; Ukraine's sheltering and evacuation response is Ukraine's scored conduct.

The 170/day figure is a structurally distinct data point. It surpasses the 2023 seasonal average and positions July 2026 as the most intensive documented sustained pace of Russia-attributed civilian targeting in this benchmark's tracking period. The EU Special Tribunal documents accountability in parallel.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Sudan: Al Jazeera 'Next El Fasher' Framing; El Obeid Day 3 Under UN Red Alert; RSF Siege Active
sectorAlerts.headline: Al Jazeera's July 6 report frames El Obeid as following the El Fasher atrocity pattern; the UN red alert is three days active; 500,000 people at imminent siege risk; Sudan holds at 0 of 100.
observations: Sudan (0 of 100): Al Jazeera's July 6 report explicitly draws a comparison between El Obeid and El Fasher — the site that produced Amnesty International's crimes-against-humanity finding in the July series. The framing documents a humanitarian-news consensus forming around El Obeid as the next major atrocity geography.

The UN red alert (July 3) is now three days on record. 500,000 people remain at imminent siege risk in North Kordofan's state capital alongside 105,000 IDPs in surrounding zones. Al-Burhan has not accepted a ceasefire. The Amnesty El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report stands from July 3.

Both evidence packages are RSF (non-state) conduct. Sudan at 0 of 100 reflects the Sudanese government's own prior conduct: the breakdown of state protection and Al-Burhan's documented rejection of ceasefire proposals. The scored upgrade trigger — an implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed access — has not occurred.

The structural condition: Al Jazeera's framing is not a scored event. But it documents the humanitarian-news consensus that El Obeid is following the El Fasher playbook. If El Obeid follows El Fasher to a confirmed atrocity finding, the record will show the signal was on record by July 6. OCHA's documentation of the El Obeid siege evolution is the next critical evidence checkpoint.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — DRC: Ebola Fastest 3-Day Growth; 1,561 Cases; July Peak Window Active
sectorAlerts.headline: The DRC's Ebola outbreak reaches 1,561 cases and 506 deaths on July 6 — the fastest 3-day growth in the outbreak's history; July natural peak window is active through July 31; DRC holds at 2.3 of 100.
observations: DRC (2.3 of 100): WHO July 6 count: 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths — up from 1,406 cases and 438 deaths on July 3. The 155-case 3-day increment is the fastest in the outbreak's history. The outbreak is in the July natural Bundibugyo seasonal peak window.

No approved Bundibugyo vaccine exists. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. Multi-continent spread (Uganda, France, Germany) confirmed in the prior cycle. The acceleration rate warrants close monitoring of WHO response-corridor access through the July 31 peak window.

The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. The acceleration in case counts does not by itself constitute evidence of obstruction; it could reflect natural seasonal peak dynamics. The trigger condition is specific to documented obstruction of response capacity.

The structural observation: DRC at 2.3 of 100 is the highest-composite near-floor entity in the July series being watched for a floor-crossing event. The July 31 peak-window close is the next structured forward trigger. A South Sudan Ebola incursion from Haut-Uele province remains an independent cross-floor trigger for South Sudan.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — El Salvador: Downgrade Applied July 5; Score Now 15.0 Critical; Queue Clear
sectorAlerts.headline: El Salvador's score is now 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band — the downgrade was applied July 5; the pending queue is clear for the first time since July 2.
observations: El Salvador (15.0 of 100 Critical): The July 2, 2026 proposal to move El Salvador from 20.3 of 100 (Developing) to 15.0 of 100 (Critical) — delta -5.3 — was applied on July 5, 2026. This is the first applied score change since the June series (prior apply: Humana June 21). El Salvador's published score in all public-facing systems is now 15.0 of 100.

El Salvador drops from the July priority cluster. No July 6 assessment of El Salvador was conducted. The proposal was based on: Cristosal exile, indefinite re-election amendment, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences, 140-plus rights defenders fled. The four-day carry-forward period (July 2-5) demonstrates the queue's function: editorial review, not evidence accumulation.

The pending queue is now empty entering July 7. This is the first clean slate since July 2. The queue-clear state means the July 7 cycle opens with no carry-forward obligations. El Salvador at 15.0 of 100 is 5.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0.

Upgrade triggers from the new Critical-band position: due-process restoration, civil-society reinstatement, and reversal of the indefinite re-election amendment. The 2027 election under the July 2025 indefinite re-election amendment is the next structured forward trigger. A further mass-detention escalation or rights-defender imprisonment constitutes additional downward pressure.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Israel: New Perpetrator Entrant; Attribution Architecture Explicit; Floor Confirmed
sectorAlerts.headline: Israel enters the July priority cluster on perpetrator own-conduct — the July 6 al-Mawasi drone strike and Lebanon Nabatieh car strike are Israel's own realized conduct; floor confirmed at 0 of 100; attribution architecture for the Middle East cluster is now explicit.
observations: Israel (0 of 100): The July 6 al-Mawasi drone strike targeted displaced people in a humanitarian zone. The Nabatieh car strike in southern Lebanon killed four. Both events are Israel's own realized conduct, scored against Israel as the perpetrator. The al-Mawasi drone strike on a designated displacement zone would constitute a scored downgrade trigger for any entity above its floor. Israel is already at 0 of 100.

Israel's entry as a perpetrator own-conduct entity completes the attribution architecture for the Middle East cluster: Israel (0 of 100) is scored on Israel's own perpetrator conduct. Lebanon (17.2 of 100) is scored on Lebanon's own state conduct — IDF strikes on Lebanon are attributed to Israel, not scored against Lebanon. Palestine (25.0 of 100) is scored on Palestinian-authority own conduct — killings by Israeli forces are attributed to Israel, not scored against Palestinian authority.

The architecture is now explicit in the July 6 cycle. All three entities appear in the priority cluster simultaneously for the first time. The methodology's victim/perpetrator convention is being applied at full complexity: one perpetrator (Israel) simultaneously affects two non-perpetrator entities (Lebanon, Palestine) while being scored separately on its own conduct.

Israel at 0 of 100 floor. No upgrade trigger has been identified. Verified halt of strikes on civilians, restoration of humanitarian access, and withdrawal from occupied areas are the scored positive-conduct conditions.

sectorAlerts.sector: Methodology — 15 False-Positive Screens (Fourth Consecutive Record-Tied Cycle); Rank Corrections; Sahel Day 13
sectorAlerts.headline: Fifteen false-positive screens tie the series record for the fourth consecutive cycle — the first four-cycle streak; all seven FP categories simultaneously active for the fourth straight night; 60 screens across four cycles with zero incorrect score moves.
observations: The 15 false-positive screens match the series record (July 3, 4, 5, 6). The July 3-4-5-6 streak is the first time the record has been tied on four consecutive cycles. Breakdown: attribution/other-state (Ukraine, Lebanon, Palestine); non-state (Sudan RSF, Mali JNIM, Nigeria JNIM/ISWAP, Haiti gangs); exogenous (Venezuela quake, DRC Ebola); pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth); double-count (China Article 63, Nigeria Phase 5, Iran June executions); floor-limitation (Russia, Sudan, Israel at floor); floor-limitation exogenous (South Sudan Ebola spillover).

All seven false-positive categories were active simultaneously for the fourth consecutive cycle. All 15 screens resolved to confirmation without exception. 60 false-positive signals were correctly screened across four cycles without a single incorrect score move. Framework stability under maximum simultaneous pressure is validated across a four-cycle streak.

Four rotation-state rank-only corrections this cycle: China (148 to 147), Nigeria (150 to 149), Venezuela (151 to 150), Lebanon (153 to 152). These rank drifts were produced by the 2026-07-05 El Salvador apply not fully propagating downstream rank updates. Composites and bands were already correct. No proposed or unapplied composite values were written to rotation-state.

The Sahel calibration flag (Mali 12.5 versus Burkina Faso 6.3) is in its thirteenth consecutive day without a coordinator review timeline. The JNIM July 4 attacks (non-state) do not resolve the state-conduct calibration question. At thirteen days, this is now a methodology-governance item rather than a calibration note.

methodologyNotes.name: False-Positive Attribution Framework — 15 Screens (Fourth Consecutive Record-Tied Cycle; First Four-Cycle Streak)
methodologyNotes.description: Fifteen false-positive screens were applied this cycle — tying the series record for the fourth consecutive cycle. First four-cycle record streak in benchmark history. 60 total screens across four cycles (July 3-6) with zero incorrect score moves. Seven active categories: (1) attribution/other-state (Ukraine: Jul 5-6 Kyiv/Dnipro scored against Russia; Lebanon: Jul 6 IDF Nabatieh strike scored against Israel; Palestine: Jul 6 killings scored against Israel); (2) non-state (Sudan: RSF El Obeid siege; Mali: JNIM; Nigeria: JNIM/ISWAP; Haiti: gangs); (3) exogenous (Venezuela: earthquake; DRC: Ebola disease); (4) pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth: DOJ probes not adjudicated); (5) double-count (China: Article 63 Day 6 already-priced; Nigeria: Phase 5 already-priced June 19; Iran: 141 June executions continue priced 784+ YTD pattern); (6) floor-limitation with own-conduct correctly attributed (Russia: Jul 5-6 strikes own-conduct, at floor; Israel: al-Mawasi + Nabatieh own-conduct, at floor); (7) floor-limitation with exogeneity (South Sudan: famine reinforces floor; Ebola spillover is exogenous). All 15 correctly disposed of without exception.

methodologyNotes.name: El Salvador Apply — Score Updated to 15.0 of 100 Critical; Queue Clear; First Apply Since June Series
methodologyNotes.description: The 2026-07-02 downgrade proposal (El Salvador 20.3 to 15.0, Developing to Critical, delta -5.3, confidence: medium) was applied on 2026-07-05. Published score is now 15.0 of 100, Critical band, rank 155. This was the first applied score change since the June series (prior apply: Humana 2026-06-21). The four-day carry-forward period (July 2-5) demonstrates the queue's function. Proposal basis: Cristosal exile, indefinite re-election amendment, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences, 140-plus rights defenders fled. The pending queue entered July 6 empty and exits July 6 empty. El Salvador dropped from the priority cluster.

methodologyNotes.name: Rotation-State Rank Corrections — Four This Cycle (Rank-Only; Composites Already Correct)
methodologyNotes.description: Four pre-existing rank-only drifts corrected to published index values: (1) China rank 148 to 147 (composite 19.5 correct); (2) Nigeria rank 150 to 149 (composite 18.0 correct); (3) Venezuela rank 151 to 150 (composite 18.0 correct); (4) Lebanon rank 153 to 152 (composite 17.2 correct). These rank drifts were produced by the 2026-07-05 El Salvador apply not fully propagating all downstream rank updates across entities ranked near El Salvador's new position at rank 155. Composites and bands were already correct. No proposed or unapplied composite values were written to rotation-state. The corrections confirm that the July 5 apply did not introduce composite errors.

methodologyNotes.name: Israel Entry as Perpetrator Own-Conduct Entity — Attribution Architecture Explicit for Middle East Cluster
methodologyNotes.description: Israel enters the July priority cluster for the first time as a perpetrator own-conduct entity, confirmed at 0 of 100 floor. The July 6 al-Mawasi drone strike on displaced people and the Nabatieh car strike in Lebanon (four killed) are Israel's own realized conduct, correctly scored against Israel under the victim/perpetrator convention. The architecture is now explicit in the July 6 cycle: Israel (0 of 100) is scored on Israel's own perpetrator conduct; Lebanon (17.2 of 100) is scored on Lebanon's own state conduct — IDF strikes are attributed to Israel, not Lebanon; Palestine (25.0 of 100) is scored on Palestinian-authority own conduct — killings by Israeli forces are attributed to Israel, not Palestinian authority. The al-Mawasi drone strike on a designated displacement zone would constitute a scored downgrade trigger for any entity above its floor.

forwardTriggers.trigger: 14-day reassessment checkpoint — independently corroborated suppression or falsification of casualty data, or documented deliberate aid obstruction, is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Official toll 3,300-plus on Day 12.

Close of Khamenei state funeral window — documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures during July 4-9, constitutes a new scored harm event from 2.5 of 100. 141 June executions are on record as the highest monthly total in the priced arc. Burial is July 9.

TPS work-authorization expiration for approximately 350,000 Haitians — United States-conduct forward checkpoint. Documented Haitian-state security-force atrocities or deliberate relief obstruction remains the Haiti-scored downgrade trigger from 4.7 of 100.

US-Iran Doha nuclear talks scheduled to resume July 11. A formal agreement with verifiable compliance would constitute scored positive own-conduct; a breakdown combined with new provocation would be a scored harm event.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

Q2 2026 earnings call — material DOJ disclosures, investor-facing admissions about probe scope, indictment, or settlement announcements are scored triggers under Accountability and Integrity dimensions.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window closes. 1,561 cases and 506 deaths on July 6 — fastest 3-day growth in outbreak history. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI.
EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. 42-state attorney general proceedings ongoing.
EU AI Act full applicability — 27 days away. Anthropic compliance infrastructure is documented. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next forward factor.
August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and actual WFP food-assistance cuts are scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa at IPC AMN Phase 5. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration combined with documented government aid obstruction is a scored change trigger.

2027 election under the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment. Enforcement of the amendment in the 2027 election is the next structured forward trigger. El Salvador now at 15.0 of 100 Critical (applied July 5, 2026). Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.

First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member. Law entered force July 1; six days without enforcement as of July 6.

Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Cessation of deliberate attacks on civilians, return of forcibly deported children, and cooperation with the EU Special Tribunal are the upgrade-trigger conditions. July civilian-casualty rate at 170 per day — the highest since 2023.

An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access is the scored positive own-conduct trigger. El Obeid and El Fasher are the dual sentinel geographies. Al Jazeera July 6 frames El Obeid as the next El Fasher — UN red alert Day 3.

Verified halt of strikes on civilians, restoration of humanitarian access, withdrawal from occupied areas, and documented cooperation with accountability mechanisms are the scored positive-conduct conditions from the 0 of 100 floor.
A new ceasefire AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon AND reconstruction delivery are the scored positive-conduct triggers from 17.2 of 100.

A realized Ebola incursion from DRC Haut-Uele province combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute a scorable own-conduct event — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.
Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100). Thirteenth consecutive day without a timeline.

Independently documented Palestinian-authority deliberate targeting of, or systematic aid diversion from, its own civilians is the downgrade trigger from 25.0 of 100. Verified equitable civilian-protection or relief scale-up under Palestinian control is the upgrade trigger.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 with 141 June executions on record as the Khamenei burial approaches July 9. If the funeral window closes without a documented crackdown, does the June monthly execution count constitute new scored conduct on the Accountability dimension — or does the double-count rule hold?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Iran's Accountability dimension reflects a priced 784-plus execution arc. The 141-execution June count is new monthly evidence within that arc.
